title: Mean, Median, and Mode
contentObjective: I can find the mean, median, and mode of a data set.
languageObjective: I can explain my reasoning using the words mean, median, mode, and outlier.

turnAndTalk.question: The team scored 42, 38, 55, 41, 38, 44, 40 points over 7 games. Which 'average' would best tell the coach the team's typical scoring: mean, median, or mode?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Mean, median, and mode each describe the center of the data in a different way.

stems.en: I would use the ___ because it best shows the typical score.
stems.es: Usaría la ___ porque muestra mejor el puntaje típico.

stems.en: The ___ is the most common score, which is ___.
stems.es: La ___ es el puntaje más común, que es ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students distinguish the three measures and justify a choice, noting the mode (38) is the most repeated and the mean/median sit near the center.
turnAndTalk.extend: Justify: which measure would change the most if one game's 55 were actually 100? Explain why.
extendStems: The ___ would change most because it uses ___.
The mode and median would stay the same because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: After ordering the scores on the number line, how do you find the median, and how is it different from the mean you calculated?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The median is the middle of ordered data; the mean is the equal-share total.

stems.en: To find the median, I first ___ the scores, then pick the ___.
stems.es: Para hallar la mediana, primero ___ los puntajes y luego elijo el ___.

stems.en: The mean is different because I have to ___ and ___.
stems.es: La media es diferente porque tengo que ___ y ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: A strong answer orders the data, finds the middle value as the median, and explains the mean is found by summing all values and dividing.
turnAndTalk.extend: Compute and compare: find the mean and median of 42, 38, 55, 41, 38, 44, 40. Are they close? What does that tell you?
extendStems: The mean is ___ and the median is ___.
They are close/far apart, which tells me the data is ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Quiz scores were 85, 92, 78, 88, 92, 95, 72, 88, 92, 84. The teacher says 'most scored around 87'; a student says 'the most common score was 92.' Who used the mean, and who used the mode?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Different people pick different measures of center to make their point.

stems.en: The teacher used the ___, and the student used the ___.
stems.es: El maestro usó la ___, y el estudiante usó la ___.

stems.en: The ___ is more useful here because ___.
stems.es: La ___ es más útil aquí porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students identify 87 as the mean and 92 as the mode, and argue which is more useful for describing the class's typical performance.
turnAndTalk.extend: Critique: is the student technically right that 92 was 'the most common,' yet possibly misleading about how the class did? Defend.
extendStems: The student is right that the mode is ___, but it can mislead because ___.
A fairer summary of the whole class is the ___ since ___.

turnAndTalk.question: For the data set 4, 7, 10, 7, 12, talk through how you find the median.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Order the values, then find the middle one.

stems.en: The median is ___ because it is in the ___.
stems.es: La mediana es ___ porque está en el ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students order to 4, 7, 7, 10, 12 and identify 7 as the median (middle value).
turnAndTalk.extend: Generalize: explain how to find the median when a data set has an EVEN number of values.
extendStems: With an even number of values, I ___ the two middle numbers.
For example, with 4, 6, 8, 10 the median is ___ because ___.

vocabulary.definition: The average. Add all the numbers, then divide by how many there are.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El promedio. Suma todos los números y divide entre cuántos hay.
vocabulary.visual: Mean of 2, 4, 6: add them (2+4+6=12), divide by 3 → mean = 4
vocabulary.cloze: The average found by adding all values and dividing by how many there are is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: The middle number when you put them in order.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El número del medio cuando los pones en orden.
vocabulary.visual: Data: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9 → the middle (3rd) value is 5, so median = 5
vocabulary.cloze: The middle value of a data set when the numbers are in order is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: The number that shows up most often.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El número que aparece más veces.
vocabulary.visual: Data: 2, 3, 3, 5, 7 → 3 appears twice (most often), so mode = 3
vocabulary.cloze: The value that appears most often in a data set is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: A number that is much bigger or smaller than the rest.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número mucho mayor o menor que los demás.
vocabulary.visual: Data: 10, 12, 11, 13, 50 → 50 is far from the others, so 50 is an outlier
vocabulary.cloze: A value that is much larger or smaller than the rest of the data is an ___.

examples.why: It is far from the other values.
It fits in with the rest of the values.

vocabulary.definition: How the data looks: where it sits and how spread out it is.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Cómo se ven los datos: dónde están y qué tan separados están.
vocabulary.visual: If most values cluster in the center with fewer at the ends, the distribution is symmetric
vocabulary.cloze: The way data values are spread out is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: How spread out the numbers are.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Qué tan separados están los números.
vocabulary.visual: 8, 9, 10, 11 has low variability (close together); 2, 10, 25, 40 has high variability (far apart)
vocabulary.cloze: How spread out the data values are is the ___.

launch.narrative: You're the stats analyst for the school basketball team. Coach needs a report on the team's scoring: in the last 7 games they scored 42, 38, 55, 41, 38, 44, and 40 points. Which average best represents the team's typical performance?
launch.contextImage: Basketball scoreboard with stat sheets and a clipboard
noticePrompts: What are the highest and lowest scores?
Which score seems like a typical game for this team?
wonderPrompts: Will the mean and median be the same number?
How does the 55-point game affect the average?

visual.title: Team scoring — last 7 games
visual.caption: Mean ≈ 42.6 · Median = 41 · Mode = 38. Which best describes a typical game?

explore.label: Order the scores on the number line, then find the mean, median, and mode. Place markers at: A = Mean, B = Median, C = Mode.

discourse.prompt: The mean (42.6 ≈ 42) is higher than the median (41). Why? Which better represents a 'typical' game?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The mean is higher than the median because the outlier score of ___ pulls the mean ___. The ___ better represents a typical game because ___.

optional.explanation: The middle value of five ordered numbers is the third one: 9.
With four values, average the middle two: (8 + 10) ÷ 2 = 9.

approaching.explanation: 4 appears three times — more than any other value. Mode = 4.

approaching.instructions: Sort each step into the correct order for finding the median.

categories.label: Steps to find the median (in order)
items: Step 1: Order the data from least to greatest
Step 2: Count the total number of values
Step 3: Find the middle value (or average of two middle values)
Step 4: The middle value is the median

hints: You must order the data FIRST before finding the middle.
If there is an even number of values, average the two middle values.
approaching.label: Put the steps for finding median in the correct order.

onLevel.label: Match each measure to its definition.
Data: 20, 25, 30, 35, 40. Place the mean and median on the number line.
hints: Ordered data: 20, 25, 30, 35, 40. Middle value = 30. When data is symmetric, mean = median.

onLevel.stem: Data set: 4, 6, 6, 8, 10, 12. What is the median?
onLevel.explanation: With 6 values, the median is the average of the 3rd and 4th values: (6 + 8) ÷ 2 = 7.

extending.title: Find the Stats Error
extending.correctWork: Mode = 15 (appears twice, more than any other value). Mode is the most frequent value, not the largest.
hints: Mode means 'most often,' not 'most.' Which number appears the most times?
15 appears twice. No other number repeats. So the mode is 15, not 18.

extending.prompt: A basketball player scored 15, 18, 16, 42, and 14 points in 5 games. Find the mean and median. Which better represents the player's typical game? Explain how the outlier affects each measure.
extending.sentenceFrame: Mean = ___. Median = ___. The ___ better represents a typical game because the outlier (___) pulls the ___ toward it, making it ___ than most scores. The median is not affected because ___.

editableCells.answer: Both work — no outlier, data is symmetric
Median — the outlier 50 pulls the mean to 19.2, which is higher than 4 of the 5 values

connect.scenario: Your class took a math quiz. The scores were: 85, 92, 78, 88, 92, 95, 72, 88, 92, 84. The teacher says 'most students scored around 87.' A student disagrees and says 'the most common score was 92.'
connect.promptQuestion: Who is using the mean, and who is using the mode? Which is more useful here?
connect.prompt: The teacher used the ___ (about 86.6). The student used the ___ (92). The ___ is more useful here because ___.

exitTicket.stem: Data set: 4, 7, 10, 7, 12. What is the median?
